indicator_text: Transparency: are the Ministry and banks transparent enough to protect citizens' right to know?
scoring_rule: Descriptive narrative assessment per actor
source_quote: Transparency. Are they transparent enough to protect the right to know? papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:271
notes: One of five high-level aspects applied throughout the report.

indicator_text: Openness to citizen input: are there sufficient opportunities for citizens to provide input into the financial legislative process to protect their right to be heard?
scoring_rule: Descriptive narrative assessment per actor
source_quote: Openness to citizen input. Are there sufficient opportunities for citizens to provide input into the financial legislative process to protect their right to be heard? papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:272-273

indicator_text: Equality of access: how is bank lobbying organised, does it result in privileged access and undue influence, and is there equality of access for citizens and civil society?
scoring_rule: Descriptive narrative assessment per actor
source_quote: Equality of access. How is the bank lobbying organised and does it result in privileged access to legislators and undue influence over the financial legislative process? Is there still equality of access for citizens and civil society that protects the integrity of the democratic process? papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:274-276

indicator_text: Balance and public interest: how are citizen interests and financial-sector interests weighed, and is the public interest protected?
scoring_rule: Descriptive narrative assessment per actor
source_quote: Balance and public interest. How are the interests of citizens and the interests of the financial sector weighed by the Ministry of Finance and the banks? Is it done in a way that protects the public interest? papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:282-284

indicator_text: Accountability: is there information and debate about ministerial decisions, and how accountable are the banks for their lobbying positions and activities?
scoring_rule: Descriptive narrative assessment per actor
source_quote: Accountability. Is there information and debates about decisions made by the Minister of Finance to protect the right to democratic processes? Who makes the final decision in banks on lobbying positions and activities? How accountable are the banks for their lobbying and positions? papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:285-288

indicator_text: Legally binding regulations should be introduced by national law to ensure equity among different stakeholders and citizens.
scoring_rule: Presence / absence in national law
source_quote: In order to ensure equity among different stakeholders and citizens who want to influence the legislative processes and in order to constantly uphold the standards, legally binding regulations should be introduced by national law. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2879-2881
notes: Cap-stone item: voluntary measures judged insufficient.

indicator_text: Creation of a mandatory Transparency Register with a code of conduct.
scoring_rule: Mandatory register exists with code of conduct
source_quote: the creation of a mandatory Transparency Register with a code of conduct, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2883
notes: Item 1 of 5 enumerated under "Legally binding regulations".

indicator_text: Implementation of a comprehensive legislative footprint (lobby paragraaf).
scoring_rule: Comprehensive legislative footprint required by law
source_quote: the implementation of a comprehensive legislative footprint (lobby paragraaf), papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2884

indicator_text: Establishing guidelines for all civil servants and ministers on dealing with lobbyists.
scoring_rule: Binding guidelines exist for civil servants and ministers
source_quote: the establishing of guidelines for all civil servants and ministers on dealing with lobbyists, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2885

indicator_text: Transparency about Ministers' official diary.
scoring_rule: Ministers' diary is publicly disclosed
source_quote: transparency about Ministers' official diary, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2886

indicator_text: Guidance for improving the balanced weighing of different interests with special attention to the public interest and the interests of citizens.
scoring_rule: Guidance exists for balanced weighing of interests
source_quote: guidance for improving the balanced weighing of different interest with special attention to the public interest and the interests of citizens. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2887-2888

indicator_text: Mandatory comprehensive legislative footprint included in the Memorie van Toelichting accompanying the official internet consultation, clarifying external inputs received before consultation.
scoring_rule: Footprint is included in MvT at consultation stage
source_quote: Introducing a mandatory and comprehensive 'legislative footprint' that: - is included in the Memorie van Toelichting (explanatory note) which accompanies the official internet consultation and clarifies the external inputs that have been received before the period of official consultation; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2901-2904

indicator_text: Footprint is comprehensive when sent to Parliament with policy papers / final legislative proposals, covering all formal and informal inputs (not only consultation responses).
scoring_rule: Final-stage footprint covers formal and informal inputs
source_quote: is comprehensive when sent by the Ministry to Parliament with the policy papers and final legislative proposals and includes information about all formal and informal inputs, i.e. not restricted to mentioning the responses to the consultation; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2905-2907

indicator_text: Footprint explains how the Ministry has taken into account all formal and informal interactions and how the range of interests were balanced, including underrepresented interests.
scoring_rule: Footprint explains weighing and balance of interests
source_quote: explains how the Ministry has taken into account (or not) all the formal and informal interaction and inputs it received, and how the range of interests were balanced, including the interests of those who were underrepresented in the consultation process; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2908-2910

indicator_text: Footprint based on renewed legislative drafting guidelines (aanwijzingen) ensuring fullest possible transparency without compromising whistle-blower access.
scoring_rule: Drafting guidelines updated to require full footprint with whistle-blower protections
source_quote: is based on renewed guidelines for legislation (aanwijzingen) that ensure the fullest possible transparency without compromising access to the process for whistle-blowers. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2911-2912

indicator_text: Public announcements about legislation as it is being developed (e.g. dedicated webpage with summary, monthly-updated list of external inputs received, links to consultation submissions) — a 'living legislative footprint'.
scoring_rule: Living legislative footprint posted publicly during drafting
source_quote: Making public announcements about legislation as it is being developed... By making such information public — along with all links to submissions made to the internet consultation from industry associations, companies, organisations etc. — the 'legislative footprint' would be transparent from the very beginning ('living legislative footprint'). papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2914-2922

indicator_text: Decision-makers (Ministers, Deputy Ministers, Director-Generals, senior officials) publish diaries that announce as many meetings/events as possible a week ahead.
scoring_rule: Diaries published a week ahead
source_quote: Publishing diaries that announce as many meetings/events as possible a week ahead. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2928

indicator_text: Maintain a detailed website archive of professional meetings/events with external parties, recording date, organisation/individuals attending, subjects covered, documents received before/during/after, location, links to speeches, and use of social media.
scoring_rule: Website archive of meetings with all listed metadata fields
source_quote: Maintaining a detailed archive on the website of the professional meetings/events undertaken with external parties, and publishing in very short (as is the case at the European Commission): · the date of the meeting; · the organisation/company represented at the meeting and/or the individuals who attended; · the subjects covered at the meeting and any documents received in connection with the discussion before, during and after the meeting; · where the meeting took place; · links to speeches made by the Minister at events; · ensuring all speeches made by Ministers, Deputy Ministers and Director-Generals are available on the Ministry's website; · using social media to inform the public about the Minister's activities and press conferences (cf. tweets made by the Minister of Finance). papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2934-2946
notes: Item compounds several disclosure fields; explicit reference to European Commission as benchmark.

indicator_text: Improve public internet consultations by including questions accessible and comprehensible to citizens and non-profit organisations.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Consultation questions are accessible to non-experts
source_quote: Including questions that are accessible and comprehensible to citizens and non-profit organisations, and can be easily answered; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2954-2955

indicator_text: Consultation questions address the aim and desired effect of legislation, not only technicalities.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Questions cover policy aims, not only technicalities
source_quote: Asking questions related to the aim and desired effect of the legislation, and not only focusing on the technicalities; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2956-2957

indicator_text: Widespread and targeted publicity of consultations to reach diverse potential interest groups, with resources allocated for outreach.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Targeted publicity to diverse groups, with budget
source_quote: Ensuring widespread and targeted publicity of consultations which reach diverse potential interest groups. Resources will need to be allocated for this. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2958-2959

indicator_text: Information about upcoming legislation is easier for citizens to find.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Findability of upcoming-legislation information for citizens
source_quote: Ensuring information is easier to find by citizens about upcoming legislation; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2962

indicator_text: Ministry is more accessible for citizens to ask questions about upcoming legislation.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Citizen question channel exists at Ministry
source_quote: Being more accessible for citizens to ask questions pertaining to upcoming legislation; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2963

indicator_text: More interactive channels for citizens to give input.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Interactive input channels available to citizens
source_quote: Providing more interactive channels for citizens to give input; papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2964

indicator_text: Dedicated staff (e.g. Ministry spokesperson) for relations with non-financial stakeholders and citizens.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect the right to be heard
scoring_rule: Dedicated non-industry liaison staff
source_quote: Dedicating more staff, e.g. a Ministry spokesperson, to relations with non-financial stakeholders and citizens. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2965-2966

indicator_text: Mandatory comprehensive Transparency Register used jointly by ministries and parliament; non-subscribers may not lobby officials (waivers for occasional visits and citizens).
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Mandatory joint register with non-subscriber ban
source_quote: Companies and organisations that regularly interact and lobby government or parliament would be obliged to subscribe to the Transparency Register. Such companies and organisations that do not subscribe would be unable to lobby officials, though waivers would exist for occasional visits and citizens. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2974-2977
notes: Modeled on EU Transparency Register and Canadian regime.

indicator_text: Subscribers automatically subscribe to a code of conduct defined by government in consultation with stakeholders.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Code of conduct attached to register subscription
source_quote: Those who subscribe to the Transparency Register would automatically subscribe to a code of conduct, as defined by the government and based on consultations with stakeholders. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2978-2979

indicator_text: Register includes details of all in-house and external lobbyists, voluntary lobby codes covering them, parliamentary pass holders, focus issues, estimated lobbying costs (staff, housing, travel, office, association membership fees), and individuals with legal/management responsibility for lobbying positions.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Register collects all listed disclosure fields
source_quote: The Transparency Register would include details of all in-house and external lobbyists used by companies and organisations, the (voluntary) lobby codes that cover the behaviour of these lobbyists, information about persons who have a parliamentary pass, the issues that particular lobbyists/organisations are likely to focus on, the estimated costs of each company's/organisation's lobbying activities (staff, housing, travel, office costs, membership fees of organisations that lobby on their behalf), details of the individual or individuals with legal/management responsibility for lobbying positions within a company or organisation. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2980-2986
notes: Compound item; the most US-relevant disclosure list in the framework.

indicator_text: Companies and organisations update their details annually, including a short overview of lobbying activities undertaken at Dutch level during the previous year.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Annual update with summary of activities
source_quote: Companies and organisations should update their details annually and include a short overview of lobbying activities undertaken at Dutch level during the previous year. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2987-2988

indicator_text: Register should not obstruct easy access by citizens and small organisations or companies to policy makers.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Citizen / small-org access not obstructed
source_quote: The introduction of a Transparency Register should not obstruct the easy access by citizens and small organisations or companies to policy makers in parliament and ministries. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2994-2995

indicator_text: Register implementation monitored by an independent and well-resourced authority (inspired by Canadian practice).
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Independent oversight authority with resources
source_quote: The implementation of the Register should be monitored by an independent and well-resourced authority, inspired by the practice in Canada. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2996-2997

indicator_text: Adapt the integrity code of conduct (Gedragscode Integriteit Rijk 2015) to add new guidelines on dealing with lobbyists, including clear standards/procedures for communications, how meetings are agreed and organised with clarity about topics.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Code of conduct includes lobbyist-specific procedures
source_quote: Clear standards and procedures regarding communications between public officials and interest groups, how meetings are agreed upon and organised with clarity about the topics to be discussed. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:2999-3004

indicator_text: Establishment of a registration mechanism for meetings conducted with lobbyists; record can feed the legislative footprint.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Meeting registration mechanism in place
source_quote: The establishment of a registration mechanism for meetings conducted with lobbyists. This record can be used for the legislative footprint. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3005-3006

indicator_text: Definition of lobbying that makes clear to civil servants the different ways undue influence can be exerted.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Lobbying definition adopted, covering undue-influence forms
source_quote: Having a definition of lobbying that makes clear to civil servants in what different ways undue influence can be exerted. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3007-3008

indicator_text: Guidance on guaranteeing diversity among stakeholders and experts (including civil society) and on organising stakeholder meeting invitations.
section_or_category: GOV > Protect integrity of legislative process
scoring_rule: Guidance on stakeholder diversity and invitations
source_quote: Guidance on how to guarantee diversity among stakeholders and experts, including those from civil society, and how to organise invitations for stakeholder meetings. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3009-3010

indicator_text: Identification of diverse stakeholders affected by each legislative proposal and improved impact-assessment of proposals on citizens and non-profit interests, developed in the Memorie van Toelichting guidelines.
scoring_rule: Stakeholder identification and impact assessment in MvT guidelines
source_quote: Putting more efforts into identifying the diverse stakeholders affected by each legislative proposal and improving assessment of the impact of such proposals on citizens and non-profit interests. This can be developed and described in the guidelines for the Memorie van Toelichting. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3015-3017

indicator_text: Clarify the benefits and risks of legislation to the public interest.
scoring_rule: Public-interest benefits/risks documented
source_quote: Clarifying the benefits and risks of legislation to the public interest. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3018

indicator_text: Sufficient human resources to process diverse inputs and consultation responses, so responses do not delay or undermine legislation.
scoring_rule: Adequate staffing for processing inputs
source_quote: Ensuring that there are sufficient human resources to process diverse inputs and all responses to official consultations so that such responses do not delay or undermine legislation (as in the US where numerous and lengthy responses are used by the financial industry to delay legislation). papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3019-3022
notes: Explicit US comparator: warns against US-style consultation flooding by industry.

indicator_text: Clear and practical guidelines on balancing inputs from different stakeholders, e.g. to avoid the interest group with the most consultation responses dominating.
scoring_rule: Guidelines prevent volume-based capture
source_quote: Establishing clear and practical guidelines on balancing inputs from different stakeholders in order, for example, to avoid a situation where the interest group with most responses to consultations has a dominating influence. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3023-3025

indicator_text: Memorie van Toelichting sent to Parliament explains how all formal and informal inputs were assessed and how underrepresented interests were considered.
scoring_rule: MvT explains assessment of all inputs incl. underrepresented
source_quote: Ensuring the Memorie van Toelichting sent to the parliament explains how all formal and informal inputs have been assessed, and how any underrepresented interests were considered. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3026-3027

indicator_text: Civil servants and policy makers ask industry-association representatives which members support a presented position, lobby paper, or consultation response, to avoid capture by a small subset of members.
scoring_rule: Trade-association internal-support disclosure asked of lobbyists
source_quote: Asking questions, by civil servants and policy makers who meet representatives or lobbyists of an industry association and deal with consultation responses, which members of the association are supporting the presented position, the lobby paper or consultation response. This would avoid the industry association being captured by a small group of members. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3028-3031

indicator_text: Improved Memorie van Toelichting and comprehensive legislative footprint, mandatory transparency register, openness to citizens, up-to-date BNC-fiche, and improved weighing of interests should support parliamentary, media and civil-society debate about ministerial choices and clarify how the public interest was taken into account, who is impacted, and whether weighing favoured a particular stakeholder.
scoring_rule: Synthesis: outputs of all prior items must support accountability discussion
source_quote: An improved Memorie van Toelichting and especially its comprehensive legislative footprint, a new mandatory transparency register, enhanced openness to citizens, an up to date BNC-fiche and improved weighing of interests by decision-makers, should provide reasonable information for debates in parliament, the media and amongst different stakeholders and citizens about the choices made by a Ministry and its responsible Minister... Accountability discussions should clarify how the public interest has been taken into account if at all, who is impacted in what way and whether the weighing of diverse interests has resulted in an outcome that unduly benefits a particular stakeholder. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3035-3045
notes: BNC-fiche is a NL-specific instrument (briefing on EU Commission proposals).

indicator_text: Publish a comprehensive Minister of Finance diary including meetings with external parties (inside and outside the Ministry), topics discussed (e.g. specific legislative proposal), and attendees at agreed meetings (excluding events / large meetings).
scoring_rule: Comprehensive diary with topics and attendees
source_quote: Publishing a comprehensive version of the Minister of Finance's diary, as happens at the European Commission, which would include information about: - the Minister's meetings with external parties inside as well as outside the Ministry, - topics discussed at meetings (e.g. a specific EU or national legislative proposal), - attendees at agreed meetings (not during events or large meetings). papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3063-3067
notes: European Commission diary cited as benchmark.

indicator_text: Publish on a Ministry-of-Finance webpage a list of all current EU legislative processes in which the Minister co-decides, with links to BNC-fiche, EC consultation, EP, and ECOFIN sites.
scoring_rule: List of EU legislative processes published on Ministry site
source_quote: A list of all current EU legislative processes which involve the Minister in EU co-decision making should be published on a webpage of the website of the Ministry of Finance, and not only as part of the government's website. Each EU financial legislative proposal on the list should have a link to the BNC-fiche as well as EC websites including those about EC consultations, the European Parliament, and the Council of Ministers of Finance where information about the ongoing process can also be found. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3071-3076

indicator_text: BNC-fiche starts with a short, easy-to-understand summary for non-experts.
scoring_rule: Plain-language BNC-fiche summary
source_quote: The BNC-fiche should start with a short easy to understand summary for non-experts. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3077

indicator_text: BNC-fiche regularly updated to reflect changes in the Minister's position or compromises reached at ECOFIN.
scoring_rule: BNC-fiche kept current
source_quote: The BNC-fiche should be regularly updated in line with any changes in the Minister's position or compromises reached in the EU Council of the Ministers of Finance. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3078-3079

indicator_text: Public official internet consultations are organised in due time about EU financial legislative proposals and the Minister's position on them.
scoring_rule: Domestic consultations open on EU proposals
source_quote: Public official internet consultations should be organised in due time by the Ministry about EU financial legislative proposals and the position held by the Minister of Finance on such proposals. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3080-3082

indicator_text: Public consultations announced on the Ministry's home page.
scoring_rule: Home-page announcement of consultations
source_quote: Announce public consultations on the home page of the Ministry. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3088

indicator_text: Use a range of means to attract responses from academics, stakeholders and citizens.
scoring_rule: Multi-channel solicitation of responses
source_quote: Use a range of means to attract responses from academics, stakeholders and citizens. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3089

indicator_text: Memorie van Toelichting includes details of external contacts made by the Ministry prior to website consultation.
scoring_rule: Pre-consultation contacts disclosed in MvT
source_quote: Include in the Memorie van Toelichting details of external contacts made by the Ministry prior to the website consultation. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3090-3091

indicator_text: Increase in-house capacity / human resources to identify and attract diverse non-financial interests into the decision-making process, implementing the new external-contacts guidelines (opletpunt, brede blik).
scoring_rule: Resourcing for outreach to non-financial stakeholders
source_quote: In order to identify and attract different non-financial interests and their representatives into the decision-making process to advise on particular upcoming legislation, more human resources and networking by the Ministry's civil servants will be needed. This would implement the Ministry's new guidelines on external contacts which require more attention and a broad perspective (opletpunt, brede blik) from the heads of departments of the Ministry. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3093-3098

indicator_text: Analyse the balance of interests and powers (krachtenveldanalyse) represented in consultation responses, expert and consultation meetings, ministerial meetings, and other inputs; pro-actively search for missing stakeholder views and public-interest positions when imbalance is detected.
section_or_category: MOF > Ensure all interests are weighed seriously
scoring_rule: Imbalance analysis with proactive remediation
source_quote: Analysing the balance of the interests and powers (krachtenveldanalyse) that are represented in responses to consultations, at expert and consultation meetings, in meetings with the Minister, and other external inputs. When imbalances of represented interests are identified, pro-active search and research for missing stakeholder views and public interest positions have to be undertaken. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3110-3114

indicator_text: Develop and improve impact and risk analysis of potential financial legislation on social, environmental, citizens', and public interests, included in renewed Memorie van Toelichting guidelines.
section_or_category: MOF > Ensure all interests are weighed seriously
scoring_rule: Multi-dimensional impact and risk analysis in MvT
source_quote: Developing and improving the impact and risks analysis of potential financial legislation on social, environmental, citizens' and public interests. This should also be included in renewed guidelines for the Memorie van Toelichting. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3116-3118

indicator_text: Bank gives details of all legislative proposals on which it has been active, either directly or indirectly via industry associations.
scoring_rule: Bank discloses list of legislative proposals lobbied
source_quote: Giving details of all the legislative proposals on which the bank has been active, either directly or indirectly via industry associations. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3135-3136

indicator_text: Bank gives details of lobbying activities on its website under easy-to-understand headings, with an easy-to-read short overview (position, legislators approached, main activities).
scoring_rule: Plain-language activity overview on bank website
source_quote: Giving details of the bank's lobbying activities on the bank's website under easy-to-understand headings (not maatschappelijk gesprekken (social conversations) as SNS Bank does, or 'principles' as Rabobank has been doing) with an easy-to-read short overview (position taken, which legislators approached, main lobbying activities, etc.). papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3137-3140

indicator_text: Bank defines what it means by the term 'lobbying'.
scoring_rule: Bank publishes definition of lobbying
source_quote: Defining what the bank means by the term 'lobbying'. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3141

indicator_text: Publish all lobby papers and consultation responses; make them easy to find on the website.
scoring_rule: All lobby papers and consultation responses published
source_quote: Publishing all lobby papers and consultation responses: - Make this information easy to find on the website, as did the DBA/NVB. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3143-3144

indicator_text: List significant issues covered in consultation submissions with a link to a short summary, plus all related submissions and position papers, preferably with details of relevant lobbying activities.
scoring_rule: Issue list with summaries and related papers
source_quote: List the significant issues covered in submissions made to consultations with a link to a short summary (e.g. a 'viewpoint' by ING, or 'position paper' by Rabobank) along with all related submissions and position papers, preferably with details of relevant lobbying activities. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3145-3147

indicator_text: Publish all memberships of financial industry associations in the annual report (even inactive memberships); refer in the GRI G4-16 index to the exact webpage where memberships are listed.
scoring_rule: Annual report lists all industry-association memberships
source_quote: Publishing all memberships of financial industry associations in the annual report, even if such membership is not active, and refer in the GRI index on the annual reports under guideline G4-16 to the exact webpage such memberships are listed. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3149-3152
notes: GRI G4-16 cited as voluntary disclosure benchmark.

indicator_text: Submit a complete list of memberships to the EU Transparency Register and any future Dutch transparency register.
scoring_rule: Memberships submitted to register(s)
source_quote: Submitting a complete list of memberships to the EU Transparency Register and any future Dutch transparency register. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3153-3154

indicator_text: Ensure overview of memberships is available to all bank staff and provide management board with information on each industry association's lobbying position.
scoring_rule: Internal visibility of memberships and association positions
source_quote: Ensuring an overview of memberships is available to all bank staff and provide the management board with information on the lobbying position of each industry association to which the bank belongs. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3155-3157
notes: Internal-governance item, not strictly external transparency.

indicator_text: Publish information about the bank's level of engagement with each association (board member, working groups, committees, etc.).
scoring_rule: Engagement-level disclosure per association
source_quote: Publishing information about the bank's level of engagement with these associations (board member, working groups, committees, etc.). papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3158-3159

indicator_text: Personnel budget and operating costs for the unit/department that lobbies and/or develops the bank's position, including external lobbyists.
scoring_rule: Personnel and operating cost of lobbying unit disclosed
source_quote: personnel budget and operating costs for the unit or department that lobbies, and/or develops and presents the bank's position on legislation, including lobbying by external lobbyists, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3163-3165

indicator_text: Costs of membership of all financial industry organisations that represent or lobby on behalf of the bank (regardless of active involvement).
scoring_rule: Industry-association membership-fee disclosure
source_quote: costs of membership of all financial industry organisations that represent or lobby on behalf of banks (regardless of whether the bank is actively involved in such activity), papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3169-3170

indicator_text: Cost of membership of lobby industry associations (public-affairs associations) to which in-house lobbyists belong as members.
scoring_rule: Public-affairs-association membership cost disclosure
source_quote: cost of membership of lobby industry associations (often referred to as public affairs associations) to which in-house lobbyists belong as members, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3171-3172

indicator_text: Estimated costs of activities by bank representatives (from experts to management-board members) in relation to contact with legislative authorities.
scoring_rule: Estimated cost of representatives' lobbying activities
source_quote: estimated costs of activities conducted by bank representations — from experts to members of the management board — in relation to contact with legislative authorities. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3173-3174

indicator_text: Develop a code of conduct for those involved in lobbying, expertise sharing and representation, potentially incorporated into the Dutch bankers' oath; otherwise ensure all are subject to (preferably mandatory or statutory) codes of conduct.
scoring_rule: Lobbying code of conduct (preferably statutory) covers all involved
source_quote: Developing a code of conduct for those involved in lobbying, expertise sharing and representation, which could be incorporated into the current Dutch bankers' oath. Otherwise, ensure that all are subject to (voluntary but preferably mandatory or statutory) codes of conduct. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3178-3180

indicator_text: Lobbying treated as a corporate-responsibility issue: all lobbying must be in line with the bank's sustainability statements/goals, with explanation when not.
scoring_rule: Lobbying covered by CSR/sustainability framework
source_quote: Making lobbying a corporate responsibility issue to reflect the impact such activity has on society. All lobbying activity would then need to be in line with the bank's sustainability statements and goals, or an explanation required when that is not the case. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3181-3183

indicator_text: Industry associations to which banks belong have strong codes of conduct for those who lobby on their behalf.
scoring_rule: Bank ensures association codes of conduct for lobbyists
source_quote: Ensuring that the industry associations to which the banks belong, have strong codes of conduct for those who lobby on their behalf. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3184-3185

indicator_text: Disclose the names of the lobbyists who represent the bank at public institutions.
scoring_rule: Lobbyist names disclosed
source_quote: Disclosing the names of the lobbyists who represent the bank at public institutions. papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3186

indicator_text: Comprehensive lobbying policy with guidelines covering transparency (positions, activities, costs, memberships).
scoring_rule: Bank policy covers lobbying transparency dimensions
source_quote: transparency (of lobbying position, lobbying activities, costs, memberships, etc.), papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3193

indicator_text: Comprehensive lobbying policy covers integrity (which codes of conduct are followed).
scoring_rule: Bank policy specifies applicable codes of conduct
source_quote: integrity (which codes of conduct are being followed, for example), papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3194

indicator_text: Comprehensive lobbying policy covers CSR (no contradiction with sustainability objectives, no harm to society and public interest).
scoring_rule: Bank policy aligns lobbying with CSR
source_quote: CSR (no contradiction to sustainability objectives, no harm to society and public interest), papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3195

indicator_text: Comprehensive lobbying policy covers coordination and supervision of all lobbying positions.

source_quote: coordination and supervision of all lobbying positions, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3196

indicator_text: Comprehensive lobbying policy defines who is finally responsible and accountable for lobbying positions and activities, and (if appropriate) for redressing excessive or undue behaviour.
scoring_rule: Final responsibility for lobbying assigned
source_quote: defining who is finally responsible and therefore accountable for lobbying positions and activities, and if appropriate, for (redressing) excessive or undue behaviour, papers/text/SOMO_2016__structural_problem.txt:3197-3198

indicator_text: Comprehensive lobbying policy covers level of engagement in, and responsibility for, positions and lobbying activities of financial industry associations, to avoid abuses of representation and excessive lobbying.
scoring_rule: Engagement and responsibility for association lobbying defined
